folio.label: 157v
folio.l: ñaf in hire thougút , witú Inne a litel space
That in that place , after hire sone she criede
Where as he was casten , in a pyt byside
¢ O grete god , that ¹formest thy laude
By moutú of Innocentes , lo here thy mygút
This ge~me of chastite , this Emeraude
And eke of Martirdom , the rubie brygút
There he witú throte , I koruen , lay vprigút
He Alma redemptoris , gan to synge
So loude , that al the place , gan to rynge
¢ The céstene folk , that thurgú the strete wente
In comen , for to wondre vp oon this thyngö
And hastifly , they for the Prouost sente
He com a noon , witú outen tariyngö
And herietú cést , that is of heuene kyngö
And eke his moder , honour of mankynde
And after that , the Iewes let he bynde
¢ This child , witú pitous lamentaciou~
Vp taken was , syngyngö his song alwey
And witú the honour , of gret processiou~
They carien hi~ , vn to the next abbey
His moder swougúnyngö , by his bere lay
Vn ethe mygút the peeple , that was there
This newe Rachel , bryngen froo his bere
¢ Witú turment , and witú shameful detú echo~
This Prouest , dotú the Iewes for to sterue
That of this morder wist , & that a noon
He nolde no swicú cursednesse , obserue
Iuel shal haue , that euel wol deserue
Therfore w wilde hors , he dede he~ drawe
And after that , he hengö he~ by the lawe
¢ Vp on this bere , ay litú this Innocent
Biforn~ the chef auter , while the masse laste
And after that , the Abbot w his couent
Han sped hem , to burien hi~ ful faste
And whan they halywater , on hi~ kaste
ñet spak this child , whan spreynt was the haliwater
And sangö , O Alma redemptoris mater
¢ This Abbot , whicú that was , an holy man
As monkes ben , or elles ougúten be
This ©onge child , to co~iure he bigan
And seide , o dere child , I halse the
In vertu , of the holy Trynyte
